diagrams.title: Hash Function Process
diagrams.description: Shows how variable-length input is transformed into fixed-length hash output through compression functions
diagrams.purpose: Understanding one-way transformation and why output is fixed-size regardless of input

diagrams.title: HMAC Structure
diagrams.description: Shows nested hash structure with inner and outer hash operations using different key paddings
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how HMAC provides stronger authentication than simple hash with key

diagrams.title: Birthday Attack Probability
diagrams.description: Graph showing probability of collision vs number of attempts for n-bit hash function
elements: X-axis: Number of attempts (n/2 attempts marked)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why 128-bit hash only provides 64-bit security level against collisions

diagrams.title: Hash vs MAC vs Digital Signature
diagrams.description: Comparison table showing security properties provided by each mechanism
elements: Digital Signature: Integrity + Authentication + Non-repudiation (asymmetric key)
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying the difference between these cryptographic constructs
